export_result.sh line 43: PORT = 6661; setup_task.sh lines 26, 39: port 6661 checks and instructions; verifier.py lines 46, 48: port 6661 references; setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 84: Docker port mapping
setup_task.sh line 18: mkdir -p /home/ga/received_results; setup_task.sh line 40: task instructions; export_result.sh line 109: FILE_OUTPUT_DIR="/home/ga/received_results"

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a NextGen Connect integration engine channel named 'Analyzer_Gateway' that listens on TCP port 6661 for HL7 messages with custom Z-segments (ZID) and writes received messages to /home/ga/received_results/. The channel must respond with custom acknowledgements containing a ZAK segment that echoes the transaction ID from the incoming message. All data used in this task is synthetically generated: the sample HL7 message is created during setup (setup_task.sh lines 8-13), and dynamic test messages are generated during verification with random transaction IDs to prevent hardcoding (export_result.sh lines 49-53). No external data sources are used.
